coaching.coaching: Pin down probe_remote exception-propagation contract with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: probe_remote exception-propagation contract

coaching.coaching: Plan how migrated test file destination paths will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: migrated test file destination paths

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the plan explicitly states that `adapters/jira/probe.py` raises `inbound_probe.ProbeConfigError` (imported from the vocabulary module) rather than defining its own, so the existing `except probe_mod.ProbeConfigError` catch in `_run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch` continues to handle missing-env gracefully after the refactor.
evidence: run_differs.py:370: `except probe_mod.ProbeConfigError as exc:` — catches from `inbound_probe` module, not from adapter

Plan says ProbeConfigError stays in inbound_probe.py ('The neutral vocabulary — ProbeBranch (4 branches), ProbeResult, ProbeConfigError — stays in root inbound_probe.py') but does not specify that adapters/jira/probe.py must import it from there
ABSENCE: plan has no statement that the adapter raises the same ProbeConfigError class object that run_differs.py catches
findings.finding: The plan does not specify that `adapters/jira/probe.py` must import `ProbeConfigError` FROM `inbound_probe.py` rather than defining its own copy. The existing `_run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch` catches `probe_mod.ProbeConfigError` (run_differs.py:370) — after the refactor this catch is still needed when the Jira adapter's env-vars are missing, but it only catches the exception class from `inbound_probe.py`. If `adapters/jira/probe.py` defines its own `ProbeConfigError`, the catch in `run_differs.py` misses it, turning a recoverable config-error into an unhandled exception that aborts the pass for that mutation.
scenarios: adapters/jira/probe.py is written with its own `class ProbeConfigError(RuntimeError)` — the run_differs.py catch misses it, missing-env now crashes the pass instead of logging and continuing
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Approach section under adapters/jira/probe.py: 'The adapter imports `ProbeConfigError` from `inbound_probe` (the vocabulary module) and raises it for missing JIRA_* env-vars, so the existing `except probe_mod.ProbeConfigError` catch in `run_differs.py` remains effective without change.'

findings.finding: The `SupportsAbsenceProbe.probe_remote()` method's exception-propagation contract is unspecified: the plan does not say whether `probe_remote` is permitted to raise `ProbeConfigError` (propagating it to the existing `except probe_mod.ProbeConfigError` catch in `run_differs.py:370`) or must catch and convert it to `UNREACHABLE` internally. An implementer choosing the latter would silently mask misconfiguration, making the existing catch in `_run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch` dead code.
findings.location: Approach section — SupportsAbsenceProbe protocol definition; run_differs.py:369-376
findings.suggested_fix: In the Approach section, add one sentence under the SupportsAbsenceProbe definition: 'probe_remote propagates ProbeConfigError to its caller unchanged; _run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch retains its existing except probe_mod.ProbeConfigError catch, which now guards both the direct-call path (pre-migration) and the backend-delegated path (post-migration).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete destination path for the migrated probe tests (e.g., `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/fetch/test_jira_probe.py` or a new `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/` directory) so implementers know exactly where the classification tests for `adapters/jira/probe.py` land.
evidence: test_inbound_probe.py:10 loads from PROBE_PATH = .../inbound_probe.py and calls probe_mod._make_request, probe_mod._resolve_env, probe_mod.classify_probe_response
Plan: 'The Jira mechanics ... move to a new adapters/jira/probe.py' — meaning these symbols no longer live in inbound_probe.py
No destination path such as tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/fetch/adapters/jira/ or tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/ is named anywhere in the plan
ABSENCE: no adapters/jira test directory is listed in scope or testing sections
findings.finding: The Scope says 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/fetch/test_inbound_probe.py, test_inbound_probe_dispatch.py (classification tests migrate to the adapter home)' but names no concrete destination path. The current test_inbound_probe.py calls probe_mod._make_request, probe_mod._resolve_env, and probe_mod.classify_probe_response — all of which move to adapters/jira/probe.py. No adapters/jira/ test directory is mentioned or created anywhere in the plan, leaving an implementer without a specified target path.
findings.location: Scope section: 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/fetch/test_inbound_probe.py, test_inbound_probe_dispatch.py (classification tests migrate to the adapter home)'
scenarios: Implementer moves classification logic to adapters/jira/probe.py but leaves test_inbound_probe.py pointing at the now-empty inbound_probe.py, causing the test to fail or test nothing.
Implementer creates a new test file at an ad-hoc path inconsistent with the project test layout, breaking test discovery conventions.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/fetch/test_jira_probe.py` (new, replacing test_inbound_probe.py for Jira-specific mechanics: _make_request GET-only invariant, _resolve_env ProbeConfigError, classify_probe_response all branches). Retire the old test_inbound_probe.py or replace it with a thin smoke test that the vocabulary symbols (ProbeBranch, ProbeResult, ProbeConfigError) still exist in inbound_probe.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact wiring mechanism for 'reuse the already-loaded instance' at run_differs.py:234 — e.g., 'delete the :234 load call and thread the already-hoisted backend object through as a local variable in the enclosing function.'
evidence: 'The later load site reuses the already-loaded instance' — no mechanism stated (parameter passing? caching? re-calling the loader?)

The plan specifies that _load_reconcile_backend() is 'hoisted above the probe dispatch' and 'passed into _run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch as a new backend parameter' but does not say what happens at the original :234 call site — whether it is deleted, replaced, or calls the same object by another means
findings.finding: The plan says 'the later load site reuses the already-loaded instance' (referring to run_differs.py:234 after the backend is hoisted above :230) but does not specify the wiring mechanism — pass as parameter, module-level variable, memoized loader, or other. An executor must choose a pattern without guidance, which could produce inconsistent implementations.
findings.location: Approach section — 'Wiring: today the backend is loaded at run_differs.py:234 — AFTER the probe dispatch call at :230 … The later load site reuses the already-loaded instance.'
scenarios: Executor passes backend as parameter to _run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch but leaves the :234 call site as-is, creating two loads and subtle ordering bugs
Executor introduces a module-level cache, adding statefulness the plan did not intend
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'The :234 call site is replaced with the already-loaded local variable; no second load occurs. The backend object is threaded as a local in the enclosing function body, not stored in a module-level variable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'byte-identical' in AC3 with a concrete proving protocol — e.g., 'the migrated test file contains the same number of parametrized branch cases as the original (assert `pytest --collect-only -q … | grep test_` count matches), and all pass.'
evidence: 'byte-identical' is a strong equivalence claim with no stated measurement protocol
'verified by the migrated probe tests' — tests passing proves the migrated tests pass, not that coverage/assertions are equivalent to the original tests
Testing section lists the same branch set (200+resolved, 200+other, 404/410/403, 5xx/401/timeout, missing env) but does not confirm these are the COMPLETE set from the original test file
findings.finding: AC3 claims 'byte-identical for Jira (all branches)' but names no proving command that establishes identity. The migrated tests passing is a necessary but not sufficient condition — they could be less comprehensive than the originals. No diff protocol or assertion count check is described.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Probe classification behavior is byte-identical for Jira (all branches) — verified by the migrated probe tests.'
scenarios: A migrated test file that drops one edge-case assertion still passes CI, satisfying AC3 as written while silently regressing a branch
An executor interprets 'byte-identical' as 'same return values on same inputs' but tests only a subset of the original inputs
findings.suggested_fix: State: 'Verified by running pytest --collect-only -q on both the original and migrated test files and confirming the branch-case count is unchanged, plus all cases green.' Alternatively, use parametrize parity: confirm the parametrize IDs in the migrated file are a superset of those in the original.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly enumerate all callers of `inbound_probe.probe()` and assert no others exist (or add expand-contract: keep a deprecated shim briefly), and document a rollback path for the `_load_reconcile_backend()` load-order hoist.
evidence: Plan identifies run_differs.py:369 as the call site and updates it, but does not enumerate or assert there are no other callers of the original inbound_probe.probe() function

Load-order hoist is described as needed but carries no rollback path: 'the _load_reconcile_backend() call (run_differs.py:62-75, a pure registry/config load with no remote side effects) is hoisted above the probe dispatch'
T4 rubric: 'The remedy is an explicit rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract — acknowledgement alone is insufficient'
findings.finding: The removal of the `probe()` function from `inbound_probe.py` is a breaking change for any caller of that function, and the load-order hoist of `_load_reconcile_backend()` in `run_differs.py` is a behavioral sequencing change — neither is acknowledged as an explicit destructive choice, and no rollback path or expand-contract sequence is specified.
findings.location: Approach section — 'run_differs.py:369 routes through the backend' and 'the _load_reconcile_backend() call … is hoisted above the probe dispatch'
scenarios: A second caller of `inbound_probe.probe()` (e.g., a script or test importing it directly) silently breaks after the function is removed from the module
If `_load_reconcile_backend()` has an unanticipated side effect at startup, the hoist changes the failure mode and there is no back-out path documented
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Breaking changes & rollback' subsection to Approach: (1) assert `inbound_probe.probe()` has exactly one call site (`run_differs.py:369`) — verifiable by `grep -r 'inbound_probe.probe'`; (2) note that the load-order hoist is reversible by moving the `_load_reconcile_backend()` call back below line :230 if startup regressions appear; (3) if any uncertainty exists about other callers, keep a one-release `inbound_probe.probe = lambda *a, **k: (_ for _ in ()).throw(NotImplementedError(...))` shim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'byte-identical' in AC3 with 'branch-identical' or 'behaviorally equivalent' and specify that the verify step is: all ProbeResult enum values returned for each input class are unchanged after migration.
evidence: AC3 text: 'Probe classification behavior is byte-identical for Jira (all branches) — verified by the migrated probe tests'
Testing section describes branch outcomes: '200 + resolved status → PRESENT_RESOLVED; 200 + other status → PRESENT_FILTERED … 404/410/403 → ARCHIVED_OR_MOVED'

'byte-identical' normally means the serialized bytes of output are the same, not that ProbeResult enum values match — an implementer could interpret this as requiring identical HTTP response bytes or wire-level equality
findings.finding: AC3 uses the term 'byte-identical' to describe behavioral equivalence of probe classification, but the plan's Testing section describes matching branch outcomes (ProbeResult values), not literal byte equality of output — the verify step references a stronger property than what is actually tested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Probe classification behavior is byte-identical for Jira (all branches)'
scenarios: Implementer writes byte-level diff tests against raw HTTP responses rather than ProbeResult enum comparisons, adding unnecessary complexity
Future reviewer rejects a cosmetic internal refactor because it changes a log message byte, even though the ProbeResult branch is correct
findings.suggested_fix: AC3: 'Probe classification behavior is branch-identical for Jira (all branches: PRESENT_RESOLVED, PRESENT_FILTERED, ARCHIVED_OR_MOVED, UNREACHABLE) — verified by the migrated probe tests confirming the same ProbeResult value is returned for each input class.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or extend an AC (or a Testing bullet) to verify that `_run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch` accepts and uses a `backend` parameter (e.g., assert the function signature change in the routing tests) and that `_load_reconcile_backend()` is not called a second time after the hoist.
evidence: Approach: 'the _load_reconcile_backend() call (run_differs.py:62-75) is hoisted above the probe dispatch, and the backend object is passed into _run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch as a new backend parameter'
No AC text references the load-order change, the new `backend` parameter signature of `_run_differs_inbound_probe_dispatch`, or verifying that the later load site reuses the instance
findings.finding: The load-order hoist of `_load_reconcile_backend()` (described in Approach as required work) has no covering acceptance criterion — AC2 tests capability-routing behavior but does not verify that the hoist actually occurs or that no regression is introduced by it.
findings.location: Approach section — hoist of `_load_reconcile_backend()`; Acceptance Criteria — no AC references load order
scenarios: Implementer omits the hoist (e.g., passes a freshly-loaded second instance instead of hoisting), satisfying all ACs while the described approach is not followed and the backend is loaded twice
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the `test_probe_capability_routing.py` description to assert that `_load_reconcile_backend` is called exactly once per invocation (using `unittest.mock.patch` call-count assertions), covering both the hoist and the reuse requirement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Review ADR 0035 and update (or add a note to) its context entry for `inbound_probe.py` to reflect its post-refactor role as a neutral vocabulary module, not a Jira probe implementation.
evidence: Plan quotes ADR 0035 as authority for location constraint
Plan removes all probe mechanics from inbound_probe.py, leaving only vocabulary (ProbeBranch, ProbeResult, ProbeConfigError)
No plan item to review or update ADR 0035
findings.finding: ADR 0035 is cited as the authority that pins `inbound_probe.py` to the package root. The plan strips that file's content to neutral vocabulary only, fundamentally changing the file's role. If ADR 0035 describes what `inbound_probe.py` does (not just where it lives), the ADR will misrepresent the file after the change, stranding a stale reference.
findings.location: Approach section — 'The file must remain at the package root: it is location-pinned (ADR 0035 context list)'
scenarios: A future adapter author reads ADR 0035 to understand inbound_probe.py and gets a misleading picture of what the file contains
A reviewer auditing the ADR context list finds inbound_probe.py described as a probe implementation when it is now only a vocabulary module
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan step: 'Update ADR 0035 context entry for inbound_probe.py: clarify it is now a neutral-vocabulary module (ProbeBranch/ProbeResult/ProbeConfigError); the Jira probe mechanics live in adapters/jira/probe.py.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or AGENTS.md section documenting the SupportsAbsenceProbe capability contract: its purpose, the probe_remote signature, the degradation behavior (no capability → UNREACHABLE), and the rejected scalar-port-injection alternative.
evidence: Plan introduces SupportsAbsenceProbe as a new protocol in _backend.py alongside existing SupportsLinks/SupportsComments/SupportsIncremental
Plan documents a rejected alternative (scalar port injection), indicating this was a genuine design choice
No scope or testing item mentions ADR creation or AGENTS.md update
Future adapter authors need to know: (a) the capability exists, (b) probe_remote's expected return type, (c) the degradation contract (no capability → UNREACHABLE)
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new backend capability contract (`SupportsAbsenceProbe`) and explicitly discusses a rejected alternative (scalar-port injection), representing a non-trivial architectural decision. No ADR or AGENTS.md/design-doc update is planned to capture the decision or guide future adapter authors implementing the capability.
findings.location: Approach section — 'A new @runtime_checkable capability Protocol SupportsAbsenceProbe'; Rejected alternative section
scenarios: A second-backend implementer reads _backend.py and must reverse-engineer the probe_remote contract from code alone
The degradation contract (capability-lacking → UNREACHABLE) is undocumented outside the plan ticket, so it may be misunderstood or inadvertently broken

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `reconcile_helpers.py` to the consumer enumeration in the plan (as a reader of `inbound_probe.ProbeBranch` via `_load` at line 317) and confirm it requires no edit since the vocabulary symbols stay in root `inbound_probe.py`.
evidence: reconcile_helpers.py:317: probe_mod = _load('inbound_probe', 'inbound_probe.py')
reconcile_helpers.py:323: if branch == probe_mod.ProbeBranch.ARCHIVED_OR_MOVED
reconcile_helpers.py:340: if branch == probe_mod.ProbeBranch.PRESENT_RESOLVED
reconcile_helpers.py:344: if branch == probe_mod.ProbeBranch.PRESENT_FILTERED
reconcile_helpers.py:350: if branch == probe_mod.ProbeBranch.UNREACHABLE
Plan says vocabulary (ProbeBranch, ProbeResult) stays in root inbound_probe.py — so reconcile_helpers.py needs no edit, but it IS a consumer that should be acknowledged
findings.finding: The Scope omits `reconcile_helpers.py` from the edit list even though the plan cites `reconcile_helpers.py:317` as a second `_load('inbound_probe', 'inbound_probe.py')` site, yet the plan's AC1 requires the extended AST gate in test_backend_neutrality.py to assert zero adapters.jira imports in inbound_probe.py. After the migration, reconcile_helpers.py:317 still loads inbound_probe.py and calls `probe_mod.ProbeBranch` — which is correct since vocabulary stays. However, reconcile_helpers.py also currently accesses probe_mod.ProbeBranch at lines 323/340/344/350, which are the vocabulary constants that REMAIN in inbound_probe.py. This is fine. But the plan does NOT enumerate reconcile_helpers.py as a consumer that was verified, which risks a reviewer not checking it.
findings.location: Scope section (reconcile_helpers.py absent); Approach section: 'loaded by filename at reconcile_helpers.py:317'
scenarios: A future maintainer relocates ProbeBranch out of inbound_probe.py without realizing reconcile_helpers.py depends on it, because reconcile_helpers.py was never enumerated as a consumer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the transit-protection posture of the outbound Jira REST call in `adapters/jira/probe.py`: either assert/document that `jira_url` must be HTTPS (raising `ProbeConfigError` on an `http://` URL), or explicitly state the scheme is caller-controlled and document the risk in the module docstring.
evidence: Plan: 'URL construction, basic-auth GET... move to a new adapters/jira/probe.py'
Existing inbound_probe.py:52: url = f"{jira_url.rstrip('/')}/rest/api/2/issue/{issue_key}?fields=status,resolution" — no scheme enforcement
Existing inbound_probe.py:56-57: Basic Auth header built from user:token — credential travels on every request regardless of scheme
acli_subprocess.py:84: 'The SECRET JIRA_API_TOKEN is read from the environment ONLY' — sound lifecycle, but scheme enforcement absent
Plan Testing / AC sections: no mention of HTTPS enforcement or scheme validation in the new adapter
findings.finding: The plan relocates URL construction and Basic Auth credential transmission into adapters/jira/probe.py but does not declare the transit-protection posture of the outbound Jira REST call — specifically, it does not specify that jira_url must be HTTPS. A JIRA_URL configured as http:// would transmit the API token in plaintext over a potentially untrusted network.
findings.location: Approach section: 'The Jira mechanics (URL construction, basic-auth GET, GET-only invariant, status-name classification, JIRA_* env resolution) move to a new adapters/jira/probe.py'
scenarios: Operator sets JIRA_URL=http://jira.internal/... (plain HTTP, common in on-prem installs); the probe transmits the API token as a base64-encoded Basic Auth header over cleartext HTTP, exposing credentials to any on-path observer.
The new probe.py is the first dedicated file for this path — a missed opportunity to add scheme enforcement that would protect all future backends using the same pattern.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a scheme check at the top of probe.py's probe function: `if not jira_url.lower().startswith('https://'): raise ProbeConfigError('inbound_probe: JIRA_URL must use HTTPS to protect credentials in transit')`. If plain HTTP must remain supported (e.g. on-prem CI), document this explicitly in the module docstring as a known risk with a mitigation note.

findings.finding: The capability-routing tests described in the Testing section ('with a FakeBackend implementing SupportsAbsenceProbe, run_differs uses the backend's probe'; 'with a FakeBackend lacking it, the mutation is classified UNREACHABLE') have no named test file in the Scope list. The Scope names only two test files (`test_inbound_probe.py`, `test_inbound_probe_dispatch.py`) and says they are *migrated to the adapter home* — but these are probe-classification tests, not routing tests. An executing agent has no designated home for the new routing tests and may omit them or place them inconsistently.
findings.location: Scope (test files bullet) and Testing section (capability-routing paragraph)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_run_differs_probe_routing.py (new: capability-routing unit tests — FakeBackend with/without SupportsAbsenceProbe)`. Update AC2 to read: 'verified by `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_run_differs_probe_routing.py` (both routing scenarios) collected and green under `make test`.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
